scope: Initial claim-to-source mapping derived from the SENEA Diligence plan. Claims and sources reflect anchors documented in the plan; primary-source retrieval and pass/fail verification are deferred to downstream tasks (financial-rebuild, industry-attack, attribution).

claims.claimText: Seneca Foods (SENEA) trades below tangible book value on a FIFO-adjusted basis.
claims.verificationMethod: Reconstruct tangible book value from the Seneca FY2025 10-K balance sheet, add the disclosed LIFO reserve to inventory, subtract goodwill and intangibles, and compare per-share equity to the prevailing market price.

claims.claimText: Seneca trades at or below net current asset value (NCAV) when inventory is restated to FIFO.
claims.verificationMethod: Compute NCAV (current assets minus total liabilities) from the FY2025 10-K with and without the LIFO reserve restatement, and compare to market cap.

claims.claimText: Seneca's reported earnings materially understate FIFO earnings power because of a $359.3m LIFO reserve.
claims.verificationMethod: Pull the LIFO reserve and FIFO-adjusted earnings/EBITDA disclosures from the FY2025 10-K and reconcile to management's non-GAAP bridge; recompute FIFO net income across cycles.

claims.claimText: Seneca has compounded book value per share over a 20-year window despite minimal sell-side coverage.
claims.verificationMethod: Rebuild diluted share count and book value per share from 10-Ks across FY2005-FY2025; compute CAGR and compare against management commentary.

claims.claimText: U.S. private-label vegetable canning is an oligopoly in which Seneca and one competitor (Lakeside Foods) account for roughly 90% of supply.
claims.verificationMethod: Cross-check the Lakeside public statement on private-label market share against Seneca 10-K segment disclosures and any third-party industry data; confirm the cited 90% figure and identify the second named competitor.

claims.claimText: Del Monte Pacific deconsolidated its U.S. Del Monte Foods business effective May 2025 and that subsidiary subsequently filed for Chapter 11, removing destructive private-label competition.
claims.verificationMethod: Verify the deconsolidation and discontinued-operations classification in Del Monte Pacific's FY2025 annual report and confirm the U.S. Chapter 11 filing via court records or company press release.

claims.claimText: SENEA was deleted from the S&P SmallCap 600 in July 2023, producing forced selling pressure independent of fundamentals.
claims.verificationMethod: Confirm the deletion via the S&P Dow Jones Indices announcement and reconcile the effective date with index ETF rebalance flows; tie to event-study windows.

claims.claimText: Seneca has executed material share repurchases relative to float, supporting per-share compounding.
claims.verificationMethod: Tabulate buyback authorizations and executions from 10-K Item 5/Item 7 disclosures and proxy statements; compute net repurchase as a percentage of beginning shares outstanding by year.

claims.claimText: Seneca has minimal sell-side analyst coverage, contributing to the discount.
claims.verificationMethod: Pull active-coverage analyst counts from Seneca investor relations disclosures and consensus-estimate vendors; document the absence of formal sell-side reports.

claims.claimText: Seneca operates a dual-class share structure with concentrated insider/family voting control.
claims.verificationMethod: Read the FY2025 proxy statement (DEF 14A) and 10-K share-class disclosures; document Class A vs Class B voting rights and beneficial ownership of insiders.

claims.claimText: Seneca has demonstrated pricing-cost recovery across crop, steel/tinplate, freight, and labor cycles.
claims.verificationMethod: Decompose revenue and gross profit across FY2018-FY2025 into price, volume, mix, and input-cost components using 10-K MD&A commentary; confirm directional consistency with industry indices.

sourceRegister.title: Seneca Foods Corporation FY2025 Annual Report on Form 10-K (LIFO accounting, $359.3m LIFO reserve, FIFO-adjusted earnings, FIFO EBITDA disclosures)
sourceRegister.publisher: Seneca Foods Corporation / U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
sourceRegister.urlOrCitation: SEC EDGAR filing index for SENEA, Form 10-K covering fiscal year ended March 2025 (to be retrieved from https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK=0000088948&type=10-K)

sourceRegister.title: S&P Dow Jones Indices announcement of the July 2023 deletion of Seneca Foods (SENEA) from the S&P SmallCap 600
sourceRegister.urlOrCitation: S&P Dow Jones Indices index-action press release archive, July 2023 SmallCap 600 rebalance (to be retrieved from https://www.spglobal.com/spdji/en/index-announcements/)

sourceRegister.title: Del Monte Pacific Limited FY2025 annual report and FY2025 results materials describing classification of the U.S. Del Monte Foods business as discontinued operations and deconsolidation effective May 2025
sourceRegister.urlOrCitation: Del Monte Pacific FY2025 annual report and SGX/PSE disclosure filings (to be retrieved from https://www.delmontepacific.com/investors)

sourceRegister.title: Lakeside Foods public materials referencing the statement that Lakeside and one other competitor account for approximately 90% of U.S. private-label vegetable canning
sourceRegister.publisher: Lakeside Foods, Inc. (corporate communications / interview transcript)
sourceRegister.urlOrCitation: Lakeside Foods website and quoted industry interview material (to be archived as cited in the plan's Quick Source Anchors section)

sourceRegister.title: Seneca Foods Corporation proxy statement (DEF 14A) for FY2025 with dual-class share, voting, and beneficial-ownership disclosures
sourceRegister.publisher: Seneca Foods Corporation / U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
sourceRegister.urlOrCitation: SEC EDGAR DEF 14A for SENEA covering FY2025 (to be retrieved from https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK=0000088948&type=DEF+14A)

sourceRegister.title: Seneca Foods Investor Relations coverage page and consensus-estimate vendor coverage records
sourceRegister.publisher: Seneca Foods Corporation Investor Relations / consensus-estimate vendor
sourceRegister.urlOrCitation: Seneca Foods IR coverage page (to be retrieved from https://investor.senecafoods.com) cross-referenced against a consensus-estimate vendor

sourceRegister.title: Seneca Foods historical 10-K and 10-Q filings (FY2005-FY2025) for balance-sheet and inventory reconstruction
sourceRegister.publisher: Seneca Foods Corporation / U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
sourceRegister.urlOrCitation: SEC EDGAR filing index for SENEA historical 10-K and 10-Q filings (to be retrieved from https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK=0000088948&type=10)

sourceRegister.title: Del Monte Foods (U.S.) Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing and supporting docket records
sourceRegister.publisher: U.S. Bankruptcy Court (PACER docket) / Del Monte Foods press release
sourceRegister.urlOrCitation: PACER bankruptcy docket for Del Monte Foods (U.S.) Chapter 11 case and corresponding press release (to be retrieved from PACER and the Del Monte Foods newsroom)

openQuestions.question: Which specific competitor does Lakeside name alongside itself in the ~90% private-label vegetable canning quote, and is that competitor Seneca or a third party?
Does the Seneca FY2025 10-K disclose a maintenance-capex figure or a useful proxy, or does the financial rebuild need to derive it from PP&E roll-forwards?
What is the precise effective date of the S&P SmallCap 600 deletion in July 2023, and which constituent index ETFs rebalanced on that date?
Which entity (Del Monte Foods Inc. or Del Monte Foods Holdings) filed Chapter 11, and how does that map to Del Monte Pacific's deconsolidated U.S. business?
Are management's FIFO-adjusted earnings and FIFO EBITDA reconciled in the FY2025 10-K itself, or only in supplemental investor materials?
Does the FY2025 proxy quantify combined Class A/Class B voting power held by the founding family/insiders sufficiently to support the dual-class control claim without further estimation?
